notes: Spec asserts: parent ready→working at spawn; 2 child sessions appear each with ParentSessionID=parent.session_id; SubagentCount(parent)=2 while children alive; parent stays working until LAST child ends; parent working→ready after last child ends. Recipe drives a single Task-tool turn that spawns 2 concurrent subagents reading two seed files. Generalization of the foreground-subagent cell (single child) to the parallel multi-spawn shape that production usage exercises most. Top-level verify asserts parent_linked_min:2 and subagent_transcripts_min:2.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working (spec bullet 1)
First subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/ — fswatcher discovers child 1
Detector binds child 1 → parent (spec bullet 2 for child 1)
Second subagent transcript materializes under <parent>/subagents/ — fswatcher discovers child 2
Detector binds child 2 → parent (spec bullet 2 for child 2)

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while at least one child is alive — SubagentCount(parent) >= 1 (spec bullets 3 and 4)
After the LAST child reports back via task-notification, the parent's terminal stop_reason fires: working → ready (spec bullet 5)
